items.title: Reverse Sync (Pull from Target)
items.description: Allow users to pull config changes made in Codex/Gemini/OpenCode back into Claude Code. Solves the pain point where users sometimes configure a target harness directly (e.g., on a shared machine) and lose those changes on next sync. Enables true bidirectional parity rather than one-way push.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Define multiple named sync profiles (e.g., 'work-strict', 'personal-relaxed', 'client-A') with different section filters, target sets, and adapter options. Users switch profiles with /sync --profile work-strict. Solves the problem of needing different sync behavior for different contexts without editing config each time.

items.title: Config Drift Detection & Alerts
items.description: Periodically check if target harness configs have drifted from the last synced state (i.e., someone manually edited Gemini config). Surface a warning in /sync-status and optionally send an OS notification. Solves the silent drift problem where users don't know their harnesses are out of sync until something breaks.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: Export the canonical sync config (CLAUDE.md, rules, skills metadata) to a shared git branch or repo that teammates can pull from. Each team member runs /sync --from-team to align their local harnesses. Solves the problem of teams manually copying configs across members, leading to divergence.

items.title: Capability Gap Analysis Report
items.description: Generate a report showing what Claude Code features (plugins, skills, MCP tools, hooks) have no equivalent in each target harness. Shows users exactly what they're giving up when switching to Codex or Gemini. Helps users make informed decisions about which harness to use for which tasks.

items.title: Secret / Sensitive Value Auto-Exclusion
items.description: Before syncing, scan configs for patterns that look like API keys, tokens, or credentials and exclude them automatically, with a clear warning in sync output. Prevents accidental propagation of secrets to target configs that may have weaker file permissions. Addresses a critical security gap users often don't think about.

items.title: Environment-Aware Sync (dev/staging/prod)
items.description: Tag sections of CLAUDE.md or settings as environment-specific. Sync only the appropriate sections depending on a HARNESS_ENV variable. Solves the problem of accidentally syncing dev-only settings (verbose logging, debug MCP tools) to production harnesses.

items.title: User-Defined Transform Rules
items.description: Allow users to define regex or template-based transform rules that run during sync (e.g., replace all references to ~/.claude with the target's config dir, or rewrite tool names). Solves the pain of adapter blind spots where automated translation fails for project-specific patterns.

items.title: Auto-Sync on Git Branch Switch
items.description: Install a git post-checkout hook that triggers /sync automatically when switching branches. Useful for projects where different branches use different MCP servers or skills. Solves the problem of users forgetting to re-sync after switching context, then being confused why a harness behaves unexpectedly.

items.title: CI/CD Sync Verification Action
items.description: A GitHub Action (or script) that verifies all harness configs are in sync with the checked-in CLAUDE.md. Fails the CI build if drift is detected. Solves the team coordination problem where one engineer updates CLAUDE.md but forgets to commit the synced target configs.

items.title: Plugin Compatibility Matrix
items.description: For each installed Claude Code plugin, show which of its components (skills, agents, hooks, MCP tools) can be reflected in each target harness, and which will be silently dropped. Gives users a clear pre-sync preview of what won't travel. Solves the frustration of silently losing plugin functionality in other harnesses.

items.title: Interactive Sync Conflict Resolver
items.description: When reverse sync (or team sync) would overwrite changes, present an interactive diff with accept/reject/merge options rather than failing with an error. Reduces the friction of the most common sync failure mode and helps users understand exactly what's conflicting.

items.title: MCP Server Portability Check
items.description: Before syncing MCP server configs to a target, verify whether the target harness actually supports each server's transport type (stdio vs SSE vs HTTP). Report which servers will work and which need a proxy or shim. Solves surprise failures when users discover a working MCP tool stops working in Gemini.

items.title: Project Type Starter Templates
items.description: Ship curated sync config templates for common project types (Python library, Next.js app, Go CLI, ML research). /sync-setup --template nextjs bootstraps CLAUDE.md, rules, and synced target configs in one step. Lowers the onboarding barrier for new projects significantly.

items.title: Outbound Sync Webhooks
items.description: After a successful sync, POST a payload to a user-configured URL with a summary of what changed. Enables teams to build Slack/Discord notifications, trigger downstream automation, or log sync events to a central audit system. Solves the visibility problem for teams who want to know when harness configs change.

items.title: Full Dry-Run Simulation
items.description: Run the entire sync pipeline and produce a complete preview of all files that would be written, modified, or deleted — without touching disk. Currently /sync-diff shows some diffs, but a true dry-run shows the final file contents. Lets cautious users audit everything before committing to a sync.

items.title: Multi-Project Batch Sync
items.description: Sync a shared base config (global rules, MCP servers, skills) across multiple project directories in a single command. Useful for developers with 10+ repos who want consistent tooling everywhere. Solves the tedium of opening each project to run /sync individually.

items.title: Harness Performance Benchmark
items.description: Run a standardized prompt against all installed harnesses and report latency, token usage, and output quality side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to reach for on performance-sensitive tasks. Transforms HarnessSync from a config tool into a harness intelligence platform.

items.title: Remote Machine Sync via SSH
items.description: Sync Claude Code config to a remote machine's harnesses over SSH. Users who develop on remote VMs or dev containers can keep their remote Codex/Gemini installs in sync with their local Claude Code. Solves the friction of manually copying files to remote environments.

items.title: Per-Section Partial Sync Override
items.description: In addition to global only_sections/skip_sections, allow marking individual sections within CLAUDE.md with sync annotations (e.g., <!-- sync:skip codex -->) that override adapter-level config. Gives fine-grained control without needing a separate profile for minor variations.

items.title: Visual Sync Dependency Graph
items.description: Generate an HTML visualization showing the dependency relationships between synced artifacts: which skills reference which MCP tools, which rules conflict with which target constraints. Helps power users understand why a sync produces unexpected output and debug complex config interactions.

items.title: Rollback Timeline UI
items.description: Enhance /sync-rollback with a chronological list of sync snapshots showing what changed in each. Users can roll back to any specific point, not just the last state. Gives users confidence to experiment with config changes knowing they can always return to a known-good state.

items.title: Sync Fidelity Score
items.description: After each sync, compute and display a fidelity score per target (0-100) representing how completely the Claude Code config was reflected. Weighted by feature importance. Gives users an at-a-glance quality signal instead of having to read verbose sync logs to understand sync completeness.

items.title: CLAUDE.md Sync-Readiness Linter
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md for patterns that are known to sync poorly (ambiguous section headers, Claude-specific references, overly long rules). Suggest improvements before sync rather than after. Moves quality checks upstream so users fix the source of truth rather than patching adapter output.

items.title: Community Adapter Plugin SDK
items.description: Publish a clean adapter interface and SDK so third parties can write adapters for new harnesses (Aider, Continue, Cursor Agent, etc.) as standalone Python files that drop into the adapters/ directory. Transforms HarnessSync into a platform rather than a point tool, allowing the community to extend coverage.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, append a human-readable entry to a SYNC_CHANGELOG.md describing what changed across all targets in plain language ('Added 2 new skills to Codex, updated MCP servers in Gemini'). Useful for team repos where teammates want to understand why harness configs changed without reading raw diffs.

items.title: Layered Config Inheritance
items.description: Support a global base config (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) that project-level CLAUDE.md files inherit and override. Sync respects the merge, giving each project the right union of global and local settings. Solves the problem of maintaining shared org-wide rules alongside project-specific ones without copy-pasting.

items.title: README Sync Health Badge
items.description: Generate a dynamic SVG badge (like a CI badge) that shows last sync time and fidelity score for each target harness. Teams embed it in README.md so contributors instantly know harness config health. Adds social proof and visibility to config maintenance hygiene.

items.title: Conditional Sync Rules Engine
items.description: Define conditions under which sync sections are included (e.g., 'only sync the security-scanning skill if the target is Codex AND the project contains a .go file'). Enables context-aware sync without manual profile switching. Solves the one-size-fits-all problem for polyglot developers.

items.title: MCP Transport Auto-Shim
items.description: Automatically generate a thin proxy wrapper for MCP servers that use a transport unsupported by a target harness (e.g., wrap an SSE server to look like stdio for Codex). Users get MCP tool parity in more targets without manual plumbing. Eliminates the most common MCP sync gap.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Impact Preview
items.description: When a user is about to git commit changes to CLAUDE.md or .claude/, show a summary of what sync impact those changes will have across all targets. Surfaces this at commit time (via git hook) so engineers understand the downstream blast radius of their config changes before they're merged.

items.title: New Harness Onboarding Wizard
items.description: Interactive /sync-setup wizard that detects newly installed harnesses, asks the user which config sections to sync, and performs the initial full sync with a clear success summary. Reduces the cold-start friction for users adding a new harness to their toolkit mid-project.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Integration
items.description: Integrate with a public MCP server registry (e.g., mcp.run or Smithery) to suggest popular MCP servers compatible with the user's project type and auto-configure them in all synced harnesses. Transforms HarnessSync into a discovery tool, not just a sync tool.

items.title: Shadow Sync Mode
items.description: Run sync silently in the background without overwriting target files, but log what would have changed. When the user wants to understand drift history without risking their current target config, shadow mode gives them the data. Useful for cautious users evaluating HarnessSync before fully committing.

items.title: Sync Coverage Test Suite
items.description: Ship a built-in test suite (/sync-test) that exercises the sync pipeline with fixture configs and asserts expected outputs for each adapter. Users can run it after upgrading HarnessSync to confirm their custom transforms and profiles still work. Gives users confidence when upgrading.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
